danger_signals.meaning: wrestler approaching knockout state; increased vulnerability to finishing moves
wrestler in danger zone; opponent can execute pinning attempts or continued attacks

danger_signals.visual_form: distinct animation sequence and audio cue
danger_signals.meaning: imminent high-damage attack; match outcome at critical juncture

safety_signals.meaning: wrestler at optimal fighting capacity; safe to engage aggressively
wrestler in stable state; opportunity for action or recovery
temporary safe zone; opportunity to recover or plan strategy without immediate threat

faction_indicators.color_identity: wrestler-specific attire colors (e.g., Shawn Michaels, Bret Hart variations)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: wrestler entrance theme and titantron graphics (though titantron only displays on victory)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: palette swaps indicate opponent vs. player character (same sprite base with different coloring)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: wrestling ring as neutral arena; no faction-based environmental distinction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: active wrestler sprites with animations; opponent health bars visible; ring boundaries active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: wrestlers respond to input commands; active collision detection; real-time health depletion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: defeated wrestler remains downed; winning wrestler plays victory animation; titantron graphics display winner's theme imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: winning wrestler performs celebratory animation; defeated wrestler cannot act; match transitions to post-victory state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: wrestler portraits and biographies; character selection screen with all 12 roster members (plus unused '??Mystery??' placeholder)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player navigates roster; theme music plays based on selected character

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: title screen transitions; winner theme plays (SNES uses winning wrestler's theme rather than dedicated victory fanfare)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: game returns to main menu; victory state communicated through audio rather than unique visual sequence

environmental_storytelling.example: Mystery character's grey hair, pale skin, and black pants differentiate him from other roster members despite using borrowed sprite frames; visual distinction communicates incomplete development or placeholder status
Mystery wrestler lacks finishing move animations, theme song, portrait biography, and titantron graphics—visual void communicates character incompleteness to attentive players
Mystery character switches between Shawn Michaels and Bret Hart sprite frames during certain moves, visually implying hybrid/uncertain identity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responsive to input commands; performs requested attacks and movements with full animation speed
movement speed potentially reduced; vulnerability increased to finishing moves (inferred from game structure)
executes attack sequences; chains moves together; pursues downed opponent
ceases action; remains downed; cannot respond to player input
lacks finishing move execution; sprite inconsistency suggests non-functional state when accessed via cheat codes

notes: WWF Raw (1994) uses a sprite-based action fighting game paradigm where visual communication is minimal—limited to health bars, sprite animations, and entrance theming. The game lacks complex world-state systems; instead, match state is conveyed through animation frames and opponent positioning. The unused Mystery character provides insight into how visual incompleteness communicated development status. Faction identity is wrestler-specific rather than organizational; each wrestler's unique color palette, theme music, and (intended) titantron graphics serve as individual faction markers. No danger/safety systems exist beyond health depletion; environmental positioning (ring vs. outside) provides minor tactical advantage but no visual world-state changes.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - WWF Raw SNES article)
